entries.text: Food comes from plants and animals. Vegetables and fruits grow on farms. Milk and eggs come from animals that farmers look after. Bread is made from wheat. Everything we eat was grown or made somewhere.

Healthy eating means having a mix of foods: vegetables and fruit, bread or pasta, and some meat, fish, or beans. Try to eat less sweets and crisps. Drinking water is good for you. Breakfast gives you energy for the day.
Fruits are the sweet part of plants that have seeds. Apples, bananas, oranges, and berries are fruits. They have vitamins that help you stay healthy. Fruits are a good snack.

Vegetables are good for your body. They have fibre and vitamins. Carrots, broccoli, peas, and salad are vegetables. Try different colours; each colour can help you in different ways. Wash them before eating.
Water is important for your body. You need it to stay hydrated. Drink water when you are thirsty, especially when it is hot or when you play. Water from the tap is usually safe to drink.
question_hints: How much water should I drink

entries.text: Sugar is in sweets, cakes, and some drinks. A little is okay but too much can be bad for your teeth and body. Try to have more fruit and water and fewer sugary snacks. Brushing your teeth after sweet food helps.
question_hints: What happens if I eat too much sugar
